title: Embeddings & RAG: Searching by Meaning
summary: How does a chatbot answer questions about YOUR documents? Not by training on them - by turning meaning into coordinates and letting the model take an open-book exam.

context.headline: An open-book exam for AI
context.description: RAG - retrieval-augmented generation - is how AI answers from documents it was never trained on. The question is converted into a point in 'meaning space', the nearest passages are found in a vector database, and the model answers with those passages in front of it. It powers nearly every company chatbot you meet.
context.when: Behind almost every 'chat with your documents' product
